description:
# Why

`bl remaster --commit <url>` and the per-clone `bl remaster <url>` form
both flip a repo onto a master_url hub. Both route through
`commands::remaster::commit_master_url`, which calls `state_repo::ensure`
and nothing else. Unlike the legacy name-based `bl remaster <name>`
path, the URL path never runs `remaster::reconcile`.

`reconcile` is the machinery that replays a clone's local-only tasks
onto the adopted hub history (renaming id clashes on the way). The URL
flip skips it entirely. So when a standalone repo that already has tasks
flips to a hub:

- the standalone tasks live on the project git's `balls/tasks`, checked
  out at `.balls/worktree`;
- `state_repo::ensure` materializes a fresh `.balls/state-repo` tracking
  the hub's `balls/tasks`, which does not contain those tasks;
- the `.balls/tasks` symlink is repointed to `state-repo/.balls/tasks`.

The standalone tasks are now stranded on the orphaned `.balls/worktree`:
invisible to every `bl` command, silently — no warning, no error, no
migration. bl-f440's detach fix force-overwrites `balls/tasks` with the
federated state-repo content, so a later `remaster --detach` does not
recover them either. Silent data loss on a routine transition.

# Scope / design

Flipping onto a hub is 'adopt an existing shared store', not 'merge my
store into someone else's'. Carrying arbitrary local tasks into a hub is
ambiguous (ownership, id clashes) and is exactly what the name-path
`reconcile` exists for; the URL path has deliberately never done it. So
rather than silently strand: fail hard.

- The URL flip aborts if the local store has any open tasks, *before*
  `state_repo::ensure` runs — no `.balls/state-repo` materialized, no
  config written, clean to retry.
- The error names the two recommended remedies: close all open tasks
  first, or discard the local task store so there is nothing to strand
  (start clean against the hub).
- A `--force` flag proceeds anyway, accepting that the open local tasks
  are abandoned on `.balls/worktree`.

Open design questions:
- 'Open tasks' — any non-closed task (open / in_progress / review /
  deferred), or any local task at all? The strand hits all of them;
  archived ones matter less but still vanish from view.
- Guard the URL path only, or also gate the name path? Lean: URL path
  only — `reconcile` already carries the name path's tasks correctly by
  design, so it needs no guard.

# Origin

Found reviewing bl-f440 (warm `bl remaster --detach`), 2026-05-20. The
detach side of master_url got its worktree-transplant fix there; this is
the *flip* side — the inbound transition has no task-carry path and
loses data silently. Sibling of bl-692b (the orphaned `.balls/state-repo`
clone left by detach); both surfaced auditing the master_url lifecycle.
title: remaster --commit to a hub silently strands local tasks
